fields.summary: Expose RM active/standby state to Web UI and REST API

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: Both active and standby RM shall expose it's web server and show it's current state (active or standby) on web page. Users should be able to access this information through the REST API as well.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: This would depend on the implementation choice for YARN-1027. If we dont start all/external-facing services in standby mode then this jira will not work.

We need a way to monitor state of standby RM,web UI is a possible choice,just like what HDFS has done.
Other services (ApplicationMasterService,AdminService etc.)is not a necessary to be started for standby RM.
Anyway,add dependency link to YARN-1027
Hey [~nemon]. Are you still planning to work on this? Otherwise, I would like to take a stab at it.

Hi,Karthik Kambatla .Feel free to take it. : )
Thanks
Straight-forward patch that adds the HA state information to ClusterInfo REST and Web UI.
The posted patch doesn't add the information to JMX. It is slightly more involved and thought we could do it when we need it.

{color:green}+1 overall{color}.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12622276/yarn-1033-1.patch
  against trunk revision .

    {color:green}+1 @author{color}.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    {color:green}+1 tests included{color}.  The patch appears to include 1 new or modified test files.

    {color:green}+1 javac{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of javac compiler warnings.

    {color:green}+1 javadoc{color}.  The javadoc tool did not generate any warning messages.

    {color:green}+1 eclipse:eclipse{color}.  The patch built with eclipse:eclipse.

    {color:green}+1 findbugs{color}.  The patch does not introduce any new Findbugs (version 1.3.9) warnings.

    {color:green}+1 release audit{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of release audit warnings.

    {color:green}+1 core tests{color}.  The patch passed unit tests in hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-site.

    {color:green}+1 contrib tests{color}.  The patch passed contrib unit tests.

Test results: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/2843//testReport/
Console output: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/2843//console

This message is automatically generated.

Deployed a psuedo-dist cluster, verified on <rm-address>/cluster/cluster (About page) and <rm-address>/ws/v1/cluster/info (REST API) that the haState is reflected as I toggle between Active and Standby states.
Thanks Sandy. I ll commit this tomorrow morning PT if there are no objections.

Thanks Karthik Kambatla .You are really efficient.
+1(non-binding)
Agree that HA state in JMX can be added later in another JIRA when needed.
+1 LGTM
Thanks Sandy, Xuan and Nemon for your reviews. Just committed this to trunk and branch-2.
